LanguageData.RimPrison.SetPriceDesc: Set how many work coupons prisoners need to buy an item
LanguageData.RimPrison.DeleteGroupConfirm: Delete group {0}? All prisoners will be removed from this group.
LanguageData.RimPrison.NoGroups: No prisoner groups. Create groups and assign prisoners in the "Prisoners" tab first.
LanguageData.RimPrison.WorkPriorityBlocked: {0} cannot be set to {1}. The prisoner is incapable of this work type.
LanguageData.RimPrison.RansomLetterText:
{0} has saved enough to pay the {1} coupon ransom (balance: {2} {3}).
Accept and release the prisoner?
LanguageData.RimPrison.GrantCouponsDesc: Enter the amount of {0} to grant:
LanguageData.RimPrison.ManagePrisonAreaWorkTitle: Manage Prison Area Work Restrictions (checked = disabled, unchecked = allowed)
LanguageData.RimPrison.DoorAccessDesc: Allow or block prisoners from passing through this door.
LanguageData.RimPrison.MaxDebtDesc: Maximum credit allowed for prisoner purchases. 0 = no credit.
LanguageData.RimPrison.ExpandPrisonAreaDesc: Designate cells to add to the prison area.
LanguageData.RimPrison.ClearPrisonAreaDesc: Designate cells to remove from the prison area.
LanguageData.RimPrison.BabyBedMustBeInPrisonCell: Prisoner baby beds must be placed in a prison cell.
LanguageData.RimPrison.BabyFeedingIsolation: Isolate colonists from feeding prisoner babies
LanguageData.RimPrison.LogNoFood: Oh no, we can't even afford food!
LanguageData.RimPrison.RemoveModConfirm: Confirm removal of RimPrison from this save? This will clear all prison management data, prison area, coupon shops and work assignments. Correct procedure: Pause (required), remove mod content, save and exit to main menu immediately (do not let time flow), disable the mod, then load the save. Save and restart immediately after this operation.
LanguageData.RimPrison.RemovalComplete: RimPrison data cleared. Save and restart to complete removal.
LanguageData.RimPrison.ThoughtNoFood: There's no food in this damn prison! They're going to starve us!
LanguageData.RimPrison.ThoughtIncomeTooLow: My wages can't even cover the meal costs... life is too hard!
LanguageData.RimPrison.ThoughtPremiumFood: Great! My wages even allow me to buy some good food! I want {0}!
LanguageData.RimPrison.ThoughtBuyDrug: As a prisoner I can actually buy {0}! This is pure luxury!
LanguageData.RimPrison.ThoughtAllowanceGood: Our allowance is so generous, it fully covers our meals!
LanguageData.RimPrison.ThoughtAllowanceBad: My god, the colonists are exploiting us with management and bed fees!
LanguageData.RimPrison.SelectPrisoner: Select a prisoner on the left to view details.
LanguageData.RimPrison.HelpContent:
0. Prisoner UI Panel You can drag the tab bar by its blank area, and resize the panel from the bottom-right corner.
1. Schedule Manage your prisoner groups' schedules just like vanilla schedule management.
2. Work Set work priorities per group — disable/prioritize, same logic as vanilla. The number below each work type is its hourly wage. Click the work name and wage box to set the hourly wage for that work type.
3. Prisoners Manage prisoner groups (all settings are per-group), view their balances, and manually grant coupons. The right panel shows the selected prisoner's basic info and thoughts. You can better understand how the economy is running by reading prisoner thoughts — they express their feelings about prices, allowance, management fees, drugs, and more. Note: only prisoners with "Enable Labor" turned on in the vanilla prisoner panel will appear here and be managed by the mod (auto-enabled). It is not recommended to manually disable "Enable Labor" for any prisoner.
4. Policies Manage apparel, drug, and food policies for prisoners. Prisoners strictly follow your policies. They will not actively buy forbidden drugs or food.
5. Overview Colony status and activity log. Suppression depends on prison management and treatment of prisoners. High suppression prevents prison breaks and mental breakdowns.
6. Settings Configure the following — Currency name, Max debt (prisoners cannot owe more than this), Daily allowance (coupons granted daily), Daily bed and management fee (yes, you can exploit prisoners too...), Ransom amount (when a prisoner reaches this balance, they will request release — but the final decision is yours...), Debt harvest threshold (when debt exceeds this value, organs are automatically harvested. Set very high to disable), Prison regime (Harsh — boosts work efficiency but lowers mood; Deterrence — increases suppression but lowers mood; Equality — boosts mood).
7. Tutorial Economy System: First, you need to build a "Coupon Shop" (found under Furniture) in an area accessible to prisoners. Set what type of goods the store carries (one store can only provide one type of product) and their price. Prisoners will then automatically purchase items when needed — provided the store is stocked (restocked by colonists or prisoners themselves). Prisoners will try to buy items first before eating, but be careful — they may also steal food from your storage areas! It's best not to let them access your food freezer or drug stockpile. Your prices, wages, allowance, and management fees all affect the economy. Observe prisoner complaints and praise to fine-tune your prison management. If prices are too high, prisoners may complain about being unable to afford things. On the other hand, allowing them to buy high-end food or drugs can boost their mood — if you're willing to treat them that well and invest the resources! Prison Regime: Harsh — boosts work efficiency but lowers mood / Deterrence — increases suppression but lowers mood / Equality — boosts mood. Additionally, a more equal regime lowers the suppression threshold required to prevent mental breaks and prison breaks, meaning prisoners will be more obedient. Suppression System: Suppression measures your control over prisoners. The higher it is, the less likely prison breaks and mental breaks will occur. Factors that affect suppression: Number of prisoners — more prisoners means harder management, lowering suppression. Colonists and guards — the more colonists present, the higher the ratio to prisoners, and the stronger your control. Turrets — turrets within the prison zone intimidate prisoners. Prisoner mood — happy prisoners are more obedient; miserable ones are more likely to resist. Prisoner health — injured prisoners are less capable of resistance, which actually increases suppression. Prison regime — Deterrence slightly boosts suppression; Equality slightly lowers it. Difficulty — suppression is slightly reduced on higher difficulty settings.
8. Other In RimTalk, use {{ pawn.balance }} on a pawn object to get their current coupon balance.
